justification: All 7 ACs implemented and mapped in acceptance_criteria_coverage. Collect-all-errors pattern confirmed (not fail-fast). AC5 conditional required for express with undefined-vs-false distinction. AC6 conditional forbidden for standard, tested with both true and false values. AC7 tested with multi-failure requests (2-error and 3-error scenarios). Error shape is { field, code } (ValidationError type). No external validation library. 14 tests cover all 7 ACs with boundary values.

observations.text: AC5/AC6 correctly distinguished: AC5 checks undefined (not provided) on express; AC6 checks presence (even false) on standard
AC7 tested with both a 2-error and 3-error simultaneous failure scenario with toHaveLength assertions
Boundary values explicitly tested: weight_kg 0, 1000, 1001; recipient_name whitespace-only
saturday_delivery: false on standard correctly triggers NOT_APPLICABLE_FOR_STANDARD — nuanced interpretation of 'provided'
All 7 ACs mapped in acceptance_criteria_coverage with specific test names

trials.justification: All 7 ACs implemented and tested. Collect-all-errors pattern confirmed (not fail-fast). AC5 conditional required (express only, undefined check) and AC6 conditional forbidden (standard, both true/false) are distinct and correct. Error shape is { field, code } throughout. AC7 tested with multi-failure requests (2-error and 3-error cases). acceptance_criteria_coverage maps all 7 ACs. No external libraries.

observations.text: AC5/AC6 distinction is precise: saturday_delivery:false treated as 'provided' for AC6, and as 'not missing' for AC5
15 tests including boundary values (weight=0, weight=1000) and whitespace-only name
Two separate multi-error tests for AC7 (2-error and 3-error cases)
Assumptions about undefined weight_kg and absent shipment_type are reasonable edge cases documented transparently

trials.justification: All 7 ACs implemented and tested. Collect-all-errors pattern confirmed by AC7 tests asserting exact error counts (2 and 3). AC5 and AC6 are distinct and correctly conditional: AC5 requires saturday_delivery only for express, AC6 forbids it for standard. Error shape is exactly { field, code } throughout. AC7 tested with multi-failure requests. acceptance_criteria_coverage maps all 7 ACs. No external validation library used.

observations.text: AC5 and AC6 correctly distinguished — AC5 checks undefined for express, AC6 checks presence (both true and false) for standard
Boundary cases tested: weight=1000 valid, saturday_delivery=false valid for express, saturday_delivery=false still triggers error for standard
AC7 has two tests: one with 2 simultaneous errors, one with 3, confirming collect-all pattern
15 tests across all ACs with no deviations or escalations
Assumptions clearly documented (e.g., weight_kg=undefined not validated since no AC covers it)
